subject: Databases
title: Rebalancing Partitions
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Rebalancing Partitions.

hook.text: A cluster has 4 nodes. Add a 5th node. Data must be redistributed. But the app can't go down during rebalancing.

buildup.text: Rebalancing moves data from overloaded nodes to new ones. It must happen online, without downtime.

discovery.text: Fixed partition count: create 1000 partitions across 4 nodes. Add a 5th node, move 200 partitions to it.

twist.text: Automatic rebalancing can cause thundering herds: all nodes move data at once, overwhelming the network.

climax.text: Throttle rebalancing. Move partitions gradually. Monitor network bandwidth and latency during the process.

punchline.text: Rebalance gradually. Don't move all data at once. Throttle and monitor.

quiz.question: Why is it important to throttle partition rebalancing?
options: Moving too much data at once can overwhelm the network and degrade performance for active queries
Rebalancing must complete in under one second
Throttling prevents data loss during moves
